iti92:1.1: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
iti92:2.1: “Monks, even if a monk, taking hold of my outer robe, were to follow right behind me,
iti92:2.2:
placing his feet in my footsteps, yet if he were to be greedy for sensuality, strong in his passions,
malevolent in mind, corrupt in his resolves, his mindfulness muddled, unalert, uncentered,
iti92:2.3: his mind scattered, & his faculties uncontrolled, then he would be far from me, and I from him.
iti92:2.5: Because he does not see the Dhamma.
iti92:2.6: Not seeing the Dhamma, he does not see me.
iti92:3.1: “But even if a monk were to live one hundred leagues away,
iti92:3.2:
yet if he were to have no greed for sensuality, were not strong in his passions, not malevolent in mind,
uncorrupt in his resolves, his mindfulness established, alert, centered, his mind at singleness,
iti92:3.3: & his faculties well-restrained, then he would be near to me, and I to him.
iti92:3.6: Seeing the Dhamma, he sees me.”
iti92:4.3: see how far he is!– the perturbed from the unperturbed,
iti92:4.5: the greedy one from the one with no greed.
iti92:5.1: But the wise person who, through direct knowledge of Dhamma,
iti92:5.3: grows still & unperturbed like a lake unruffled by wind:
iti92:6.1: see how close he is!– the unperturbed to the unperturbed,
iti92:6.3: the greedless one to the one with no greed.
